reason: The statement applies because the text fragment describes the story of Jehoash (also known as Joash), who was hidden and then crowned as king under the guidance of Jehoiada the priest, effectively becoming the leader of the Hebrews after Athaliah's rule. Athaliah was a usurper queen, and Jehoash was the rightful heir to the throne, making him the leader of the Hebrews in this context.
